findings.message: Contract tests verify return TYPES but not safety-critical default VALUES. ModerationPort defaults to BLOCK (correct), IdentityConsentPort defaults to false (correct), LocalizationPolicyPort.is_term_safe defaults to false (correct) -- but the contract tests only assert typeof, not the actual safe value. A future refactor could accidentally change a default from BLOCK to PASS without the contract test catching it. Recommend adding _assert_moderation_blocked() and _assert_false() checks for safety-critical defaults.

Contract tests for IdentityConsentPort do not verify that has_consent() defaults to false (deny by default). The port implementation is correct, but the test only checks the return type is bool, not that the safe default is false. Same applies to request_consent() which correctly defaults to false.

LLMPort.complete_with_tools() returns Array[ToolInvocation] (typed array), which is stricter than the ARCHITECTURE.md example showing plain Array. This is an improvement, not a regression, but ARCHITECTURE.md should be updated to reflect the actual typed contract to avoid confusion for future adapter implementors.

LLMPort lacks doc-comment guidance on Ollama tool-calling contract. The port defines complete_with_tools() but does not document: (1) how ToolInvocation schemas should be serialized for Ollama, (2) how to handle partial/malformed tool-call responses, (3) timeout and retry expectations. This is acceptable for the port interface layer but should be documented before TASK-011 (Ollama adapter implementation).

ProjectStorePort.load_project() returns Project.new() (empty project) on not-implemented, while PublishToFamilyLibraryService checks for null on line 25. The service's null check will never trigger on the default port implementation since it returns an empty Project, not null. This is a benign mismatch -- real adapters will return null for missing projects -- but the contract test should verify both the default and null-return scenarios.

LocalizationPolicyPort.is_term_safe() defaults to false, which is the safe choice (assume term is unsafe when unimplemented). The contract test does not verify this value. Same finding as ModerationPort but lower severity since term safety is a secondary check.

acceptance_criteria.outbound_ports_defined: PASS - All 11 outbound ports defined with stable, typed method signatures matching the specification table exactly.
acceptance_criteria.contract_tests_exist: PASS - 11 concrete contract tests + base class + test runner + README. Each port has a dedicated contract test verifying method existence, return types, and null/empty input handling.

checklist.hexagonal_boundaries: PASS - All 11 ports extend RefCounted, not Node. Zero engine imports (preload, Scene, Input, Engine, OS, FileAccess, DirAccess). Only domain value types (PromptEnvelope, ModerationResult, ToolInvocation, AgeBand, Project) are referenced.
checklist.kid_safety_and_parental_controls: PASS with notes - ModerationPort defaults to BLOCK verdict (safe). IdentityConsentPort defaults to false for both has_consent and request_consent (deny by default). LocalizationPolicyPort.is_term_safe defaults to false (assume unsafe). All safety-critical ports fail safe. However, contract tests should be strengthened to verify these VALUES, not just types.
checklist.ollama_tool_calling: PASS with notes - LLMPort defines complete_with_tools() returning Array[ToolInvocation], which is the correct typed contract for tool-calling. Error handling guidance (timeout, malformed responses, fallback chain) should be added as doc comments before TASK-011.
checklist.contract_tests: PASS - Comprehensive coverage: method existence checks, return type verification, null/empty input resilience. Test runner (run_contract_tests.gd) executes all 11 contract tests with pass/fail reporting and exit codes.
checklist.cross_agent_review: This review constitutes the required cross-agent review for TASK-003.

compatibility_verification.request_ai_creation_help_service: COMPATIBLE - Uses LLMPort.complete(), LLMPort.complete_with_tools(), ModerationPort.check_text(), ClockPort.now_msec(), ClockPort.now_iso(), LocalizationPolicyPort.get_locale(). All method signatures and return types match port definitions.
compatibility_verification.publish_to_family_library_service: COMPATIBLE - Uses ProjectStorePort.load_project(), ModerationPort.check_text(), ClockPort.now_msec(). All signatures match. Note: service checks for null return from load_project but port default returns empty Project (see finding).
compatibility_verification.set_parental_controls_service: COMPATIBLE - Uses IdentityConsentPort.request_consent(), TelemetryPort.emit_event(), ClockPort.now_iso(). All signatures match.

summary: TASK-003 is well-executed. All 11 outbound ports are defined with correct typed signatures, extend RefCounted (maintaining hexagonal boundaries), and have appropriate fail-safe defaults for safety-critical operations. The contract test harness is comprehensive, covering method existence, return types, and null/empty edge cases for every port. Two medium-severity findings relate to contract tests not verifying the ACTUAL VALUES of safety-critical defaults (only types), which means a future regression changing BLOCK to PASS would go undetected. Four low-severity findings cover documentation gaps and a minor mismatch between ProjectStorePort's default return and how the application service handles it. Overall: APPROVE. The medium findings should be addressed before adapters are built (TASK-005, TASK-007, TASK-011) but do not block merging TASK-003.
